SEVERAL TREES OR TREE LIMBS WERE BROKEN...ALONG WITH OTHER MINOR DAMAGE.

*** 1 INJ *** EF-2, 10.5 MILE PATH LENGTH, 150 YARD WIDTH. PEAK WIND 130 MPH. NUMEROUS WITNESSES. TORNADO DAMAGED 16 STRUCTURES IN TN, 2 IN KY, AND WAS MUCH STRONGER IN TN. MANY STRUCTURES IN TN HAD ROOFS REMOVED AND SEVERAL BARNS DESTROYED. MINOR INJURY WAS TO OCCUPANT OF ONE HOME. WAS SENT TO HOSPITAL AND RELEASED. TORNADO ENTERED KY AROUND 525 PM AND TRAVELED NORTHEAST. ONE BARN DESTROYED AND CHURCH LOST PART OF ROOF WITH DEBRIS BLOWN INTO A FIELD. TORNADO LIFTED 7 MILES WEST OF FULTON, KY.

EF-1 TORNADO BEGAN IN EXTREME WESTERN FULTON COUNTY KENTUCKY AND MOVE SOUTHEAST THROUGH EXTREME SOUTHEAST NEW MADRID COUNTY AND EVENTUALLY INTO LAKE COUNTY TENNESSEE. PATH LENGTH 7 MILES. AN EF-1 TORNADO PRODUCED PEAK WIND OF 100 TO 105 MILES PER HOUR...PATH LENGTH OF 3.5 MILES AND PATH WIDTH OF 100 YARDS. ROOF SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF...TREES UPROOTED AND PATIO FURNITURE WAS THROWN ABOUT 25 YARDS.
